referencedRules.label: Sphere
referencedRules.description: A Sphere is an area of effect that extends in straight lines from a point of origin outward in all directions. The effect that creates a Sphere specifies the distance it extends as the radius of the Sphere. A Sphere's point of origin is included in the Sphere's area of effect.

referencedRules.label: Bright Light
referencedRules.description: Bright Light Bright Light lets most creatures see normally.

referencedRules.label: Dim Light
referencedRules.description: Dim Light Dim Light, also called shadows, creates a Lightly Obscured area .

referencedRules.label: Cube
referencedRules.description: Cube A cube is a three dimensional spatial volume defined by equal measurements of length, width, and height.

referencedRules.description: Emanation An emanation is a three dimensional area of effect that projects outward in all directions from a creature or object.

referencedRules.label: Heavily Obscured
referencedRules.description: Heavily Obscured A heavily obscured area blocks ordinary sight so completely that creatures cannot see clearly into it, through it, or within it using normal vision.

referencedRules.label: Darkness
referencedRules.description: Darkness Darkness creates a Heavily Obscured area .

referencedRules.label: D20 Tests
referencedRules.description: D20 Tests When the outcome of an action is uncertain, the game uses a d20 roll to determine success or failure.

referencedRules.label: Bright Light
referencedRules.description: Bright Light Bright Light lets most creatures see normally.

referencedRules.label: Dim Light
referencedRules.description: Dim Light Dim Light, also called shadows, creates a Lightly Obscured area .

referencedRules.label: Shape-Shifting
referencedRules.description: Shape Shifting Shape shifting is a transformation state that changes a creature's body into another form or materially rewrites that body's outward structure.

referencedRules.label: Temporary Hit Points
referencedRules.description: Temporary Hit Points Some spells and special abilities confer temporary hit points to a creature.

referencedRules.label: Difficult Terrain
referencedRules.description: Difficult Terrain Difficult terrain is ground, material, or space that slows movement because a creature cannot cross it as efficiently as open, stable ground.

referencedRules.label: Heavily Obscured
referencedRules.description: Heavily Obscured A heavily obscured area blocks ordinary sight so completely that creatures cannot see clearly into it, through it, or within it using normal vision.

referencedRules.label: Shape-Shifting
referencedRules.description: Shape Shifting Shape shifting is a transformation state that changes a creature's body into another form or materially rewrites that body's outward structure.

referencedRules.label: Temporary Hit Points
referencedRules.description: Temporary Hit Points Some spells and special abilities confer temporary hit points to a creature.

referencedRules.label: Friendly
referencedRules.description: Friendly A friendly attitude describes a creature that presently regards another creature with goodwill.

referencedRules.label: Curse
referencedRules.description: Curse A curse is a harmful effect attached to a creature, object, place, or ongoing state.
